content_markdown:
The diagram shows 5 gravitational equipotential lines. The gravitational potential on each line is indicated. A point mass $m$ is placed on the middle line and is then released. Values given in $\text{MJ\,kg}^{-1}$.

Which is correct about the direction of motion and the acceleration of the point mass?

diagram_metadata.description: Five vertical parallel lines representing equipotentials with values -10, -8, -6, -4, and -2. A point mass m is located on the -6 line. The spacing between lines increases from left to right.

options.label: A
options.text: Direction: to the right; Acceleration: decreasing

options.label: B
options.text: Direction: to the right; Acceleration: increasing

options.label: C
options.text: Direction: to the left; Acceleration: decreasing

options.label: D
options.text: Direction: to the left; Acceleration: increasing

vibe_explanation: Masses naturally move towards regions of lower gravitational potential (more negative values), which is to the left in this diagram. The gravitational field strength $g$ is proportional to the gradient of the potential ($g = -\Delta V / \Delta r$). Since the equipotential lines become more widely spaced as the mass moves to the left (from -6 to -8 to -10), the potential gradient is decreasing, meaning the field strength and thus the acceleration are decreasing.
